name: Firebase Helipad
prompt:
Vietnam War firebase helicopter landing pad. Cleared area for Huey operations.

Physical structure:
- Landing surface: octagonal pad ~15m diameter, made of PSP (pierced steel planking)
  - PSP is corrugated metal sheeting, dark gray with slight sheen
  - Pad raised ~0.05m above ground level
- Center marking: large "H" in yellow/white, ~3m tall
  - Built from flat box parts on top of pad surface (offset 0.01 above to avoid z-fighting)
- Circle marking: ring around the H, ~6m diameter (simplified as 8 flat segments forming octagon)
- Perimeter lights: 8 small red marker lights evenly spaced around pad edge
  - Each light: small cylinder or sphere, red emissive material
- Approach markers: 2 amber lights on one side indicating approach direction
- Ground around pad: slightly lower, bare dirt area

Colors: Dark steel PSP (0x666666), yellow markings (0xddcc33), red lights (0xff2200 with emissive), amber approach (0xffaa00 with emissive), dirt (0x8B7355)
Orientation: Flat on ground (Y=0), approach from +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~15m diameter)
Note: Since this is large, can simplify to ~10m diameter for game scale`,
    animations: `Perimeter lights: subtle pulsing emissive animation, 2s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'Guard Tower',
    slug: 'guard-tower',
    category: 'structures',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 3000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War firebase guard/observation tower. Elevated fighting position for perimeter defense.

Physical structure:
- Four vertical post legs: wooden poles (~0.15m diameter cylinders), ~6m tall
  - Legs slightly splayed outward for stability (wider at base than top)
  - Spacing: ~2m x 2m at top, ~2.5m x 2.5m at base
- Cross-bracing: diagonal wooden beams between legs on each face (X-pattern)
  - At least 2 levels of cross-bracing
- Platform: wooden floor at top (~5.5m height), ~2.2m x 2.2m
  - Floor planks (flat boxes)
- Railing walls: sandbag walls on all 4 sides of platform, ~1m tall
  - Waist-high protection for standing soldier
- Roof: corrugated tin/metal, slightly pitched (front-to-back slope)
  - Extends ~0.3m beyond platform edges for rain protection
- Ladder: simple wooden ladder from ground to platform, on one face
  - Two vertical rails with ~8 horizontal rungs
- Searchlight: mounted on one corner post above roof
  - Cylindrical housing with lens, on small pivot mount

Colors: Weathered wood (0x8B7355), darker aged wood (0x6B5B3F), tin roof (0x888888), sandbags (0xc2a878), searchlight (0xaaaaaa), light lens (0xffffcc with emissive)
Orientation: Standing on ground, front face toward +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~6m tall, ~2.2m platform width)`,
    animations: `Searchlight sweep: slow rotation track on searchlight pivot, Y axis, sweeping ~60 degrees back and forth over 4 seconds`
  },
];

const BATCH_2: AssetDef[] = [
  {
    name: 'M151 MUTT Jeep',
    slug: 'm151-jeep',
    category: 'vehicles/ground',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 3000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era M151 MUTT (Military Utility Tactical Truck) - the standard US military quarter-ton utility vehicle.

Physical structure:
- Body: open-top steel body, rectangular with rounded front fenders
  - Hood: flat/slightly angled, with two latches
  - Windshield: folding windshield frame (can be up), flat glass
  - Dashboard: simple flat panel behind windshield
  - Seats: driver and passenger bucket seats, rear bench seat
  - Rear cargo area: flat bed behind seats
  - Fenders: rounded front fenders over front wheels, flat rear fenders
- Wheels: 4 wheels with off-road tires
  - ~0.35m radius, black rubber with olive hub
- Gun mount: M60 or M2 pedestal mount behind front seats (ring mount)
- Spare tire: mounted on rear
- Grille: vertical slat grille at front (simplified)
- Headlights: two round lights on front fenders with blackout covers

Colors: Olive drab (0x556B2F), black tires (0x222222), windshield glass (0x88aaaa), dark metal (0x333333), canvas seats (0x6B5B3F)
Orientation: Front toward +Z, sitting on wheels at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~3.4m long, ~1.6m wide, ~1.8m tall)`,
    animations: `Wheel rotation: all 4 wheels spin on their X axis, 1 revolution per second`
  },
  {
    name: 'M113 Armored Personnel Carrier',
    slug: 'm113-apc',
    category: 'vehicles/ground',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 5000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era M113 APC "Green Dragon" - the ubiquitous armored box on tracks.

Physical structure:
- Hull: angular aluminum box shape, wider at bottom, sloped front
  - Front plate: angled armor plate ~60 degrees
  - Side plates: vertical, with slight outward angle at bottom
  - Rear: large drop-down ramp/door (shown closed, angled)
  - Top deck: flat with hatches
- Track system:
  - Drive sprocket: toothed wheel at front, ~0.3m radius
  - Idler wheel: at rear
  - Road wheels: 5 per side, smaller
  - Track: continuous band around wheels (simplified as box segments or single long box per side)
  - Track skirts: not present on M113
- Cupola: commander's cupola on top right front
  - M2 .50 cal machine gun mounted on cupola ring
  - Gun shield: small curved plate around gun
- Hatches: driver's hatch (front left), commander's hatch (cupola)
- Trim vane: folding bow plate at very front (for swimming), shown folded flat on glacis
- Antenna: whip antenna on rear right

Colors: Olive drab (0x556B2F), dark tracks (0x333333), black road wheels (0x222222), gun metal (0x3a3a3a)
Orientation: Front toward +Z, sitting on tracks at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~4.9m long, ~2.7m wide, ~2.5m tall to cupola)
